items.summary: Evaluate a RAG system across retrieval quality, answer quality, and failure modes before rollout changes.
triggers: check whether this RAG system is good enough
steps: Confirm the retrieval task, dataset quality, and success metrics before running any judgment.
Score recall, ranking quality, and context fit on representative queries.
Review faithfulness, completeness, and refusal behavior using the retrieved context.
Tie weak metrics back to chunking, indexing, prompting, or evaluator design changes.
success_criteria: The report distinguishes retrieval failures from generation failures.
Metrics and qualitative errors are both included.
Recommended next steps are actionable on the current RAG stack.

items.summary: Comprehensive accessibility audit of a digital product against WCAG 2.2 AA standards with remediation plan.
steps: Identify all pages, components, and interactive elements to audit.
Run automated accessibility tools (axe, Lighthouse) for initial findings.
Test keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, and focus management.
Assess each applicable WCAG 2.2 criterion with pass/fail/NA.
Prioritize findings by user impact and create remediation roadmap.
Produce audit report with conformance score and action items.
success_criteria: All applicable WCAG criteria are assessed.
Findings cite specific WCAG criterion and severity.
Remediation plan is prioritized and actionable.
Assistive technology testing results are included.

items.summary: Design a complete brand identity system including logo, color palette, typography, and brand guidelines.
steps: Research brand strategy, market positioning, and competitive landscape.
Explore visual directions with mood boards and concept sketches.
Design logo system, color palette, and typography selections.
Apply identity to key contexts (web, print, social, merchandise).
Document brand guidelines with usage rules and examples.
Produce final brand assets in required formats.
success_criteria: Brand identity aligns with strategy and positioning.
Identity is applied across all required contexts.
Guidelines include clear usage rules.
Assets are produced in all required formats.

items.summary: Develop a comprehensive content strategy with audience analysis, content audit, editorial calendar, and governance.
steps: Research and define audience segments, needs, and content preferences.
Inventory and assess existing content for quality, relevance, and gaps.
Analyze competitor content strategies and identify opportunities.
Define content pillars, messaging framework, and channel strategy.
Create publishing schedule with content types, owners, and timelines.
Establish content standards, review process, and measurement plan.
success_criteria: Audience segments are researched and defined.
Content gaps and opportunities are identified.
Editorial calendar has clear owners and timelines.
Governance framework covers standards and review.

items.summary: copy-and-a11y-review workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: design-system-audit workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Build a design system from scratch with tokens, components, documentation, and governance.
triggers: set up design system for product
steps: Review existing design assets, components, and patterns for reuse.
Create design token taxonomy with global and alias tokens.
Design core components with consistent APIs and variants.
Write component documentation with usage guidelines and examples.
Define contribution model, review process, and versioning strategy.
Audit components for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance.
success_criteria: Design tokens are defined with consistent naming.
Core components have variants and documentation.
Accessibility audit results are attached.
Governance model is documented for contributors.

items.summary: Extract component architecture, hierarchy, visual design rules, and color palettes from a UI screenshot to build a DESIGN.md design specification.
triggers: convert image to ui ux design
success_criteria: Components hierarchy is completely mapped from the image.
Styles, colors, and layout are extracted and formatted as reusable design tokens.
The output matches premium visual guidelines.

items.summary: Deconstruct a UI screenshot into parts, spawn parallel subagents to annotate each subcomponent's styling and layout, and generate clean, responsive UI code.
triggers: image to direct ui ux code
success_criteria: Generated code matches 1:1 visual structure of the screenshot.
Responsive styling and layout break rules are applied.
Accessibility contrast and aria attributes are included.

items.summary: Create SEO-optimized content with keyword research, search intent mapping, and on-page optimization.
steps: Research target keywords with volume, difficulty, and intent data.
Create content outline with heading structure and keyword placement.
Write content with natural keyword integration and brand voice.
Optimize meta tags, headings, alt text, and internal links.
Review for clarity, accuracy, and brand voice alignment.
success_criteria: Target keywords are naturally integrated.
Search intent is addressed throughout.
Meta elements are optimized.
Content passes anti-slop check.

items.summary: Review a UI for visual quality and accessibility together so usability issues are caught before polish work ships.
triggers: check this UI for visual and accessibility issues
run a design and a11y review
steps: Inspect layout, spacing, density, and clarity against the intended product surface.
Check color contrast, keyboard reachability, labeling, semantics, and motion issues.
Combine findings into a fix list ordered by user impact and implementation effort.
success_criteria: Visual and accessibility issues are both represented in the output.
Findings are tied to concrete screens, components, or interactions.
The final list is actionable for a frontend implementation pass.

items.summary: Create valid, reliable assessments aligned to learning objectives with rubrics, scoring guides, and fairness reviews.
success_criteria: Every learning objective has at least one aligned assessment item.
Scoring rubric has clear, distinguishable descriptors at each level.
Fairness review documents bias, accessibility, and accommodation considerations.

items.summary: Align a curriculum map to assessment rubrics and learning objectives using backward design principles.
success_criteria: The alignment matrix maps every objective to curriculum and assessment.
Gaps are explicitly listed with severity.
Revision recommendations are specific and actionable for the educator.

items.summary: End-to-end curriculum design process from needs analysis through implementation, using backward design and standards alignment.
success_criteria: Curriculum framework covers all required standards.
Alignment matrix maps every standard to curriculum and assessment.
Implementation guide is actionable for the target educator audience.

items.summary: Develop a complete online course with modular structure, multimedia content, engagement strategies, and quality assurance using established online learning standards.
success_criteria: Course structure is modular with clear unit and lesson boundaries.
All content and tools pass accessibility review.
Quality assurance checklist is complete with evidence documented.

items.summary: syllabus-alignment workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Implement a backend API endpoint with request validation, business logic, error handling, and integration tests following project architecture patterns.
triggers: build the backend route for this feature
implement the service layer for this endpoint
create the CRUD API for this resource
steps: Inspect existing routes, controllers, and middleware to understand the project's layered architecture patterns.
Create request and response validation schemas using the project's validation library (Zod, Joi, etc.).
Write the route handler with input validation, authorization checks, and proper error responses.
Build the service or business logic layer with database access and domain operations.
Write integration tests covering happy path, validation errors, auth failures, and edge cases.
Wire the new endpoint into the router, apply middleware, and update any route documentation.
Run tests, lint, and type checks to confirm the endpoint works end-to-end.
success_criteria: Endpoint returns correct status codes and response shapes for all documented cases.
Input validation rejects malformed requests with clear error messages.
Authorization is enforced per the spec.
Integration tests cover happy path and at least two error paths.

items.summary: Execute a code migration between frameworks, versions, or patterns with incremental validation and rollback safety at each stage.
triggers: migrate this codebase to the new framework
upgrade this module to the new API
convert these class components to hooks
migrate from the old pattern to the new one
execute the migration plan for this module
port this code to the new version
steps: Enumerate all files matching the source pattern and classify them by complexity and dependency.
Migrate a small representative subset first to validate the transformation approach and catch edge cases.
Run tests and lint on the pilot batch to confirm correctness before proceeding.
Apply the validated transformation to remaining files in incremental batches with validation between each.
Fix files that did not transform cleanly due to edge cases or non-standard patterns.
Run the complete test suite, lint, and type checks on the full migrated codebase.
Produce a migration report with file counts, edge cases encountered, and validation results.
success_criteria: All files matching the source pattern are migrated to the target pattern.
The full test suite passes after migration.
No remnants of the old pattern remain unless explicitly documented.
Migration report documents edge cases and any manual interventions.

items.summary: Run engineering and security review on the same change set, then merge findings into one severity-ordered report.
triggers: review this PR for bugs and security issues
perform code review with appsec coverage
steps: Read the diff, enumerate touched subsystems, and note any repo-specific review rules.
Run engineering quality review and security review in parallel against the same change set.
Deduplicate overlaps, order remaining findings by severity, and separate confirmed issues from gaps.
Run available validation commands or record which checks were not run.
success_criteria: Findings are grouped by severity with concrete file evidence.
Security and engineering duplicates are merged into one report.
Unrun checks are explicitly called out as gaps.

items.summary: Create and validate a database migration with up/down scripts, data safety checks, and rollback verification following project migration conventions.
triggers: add a new table via migration
alter this column with a migration
write the migration for this schema change
generate the up and down migration scripts
plan the data migration for this change
steps: Read the current schema and migration history to understand dependencies and naming conventions.
Create the up migration with proper DDL statements, constraints, and indexes.
Create the reversible down migration that cleanly undoes the up migration.
Evaluate the migration for destructive operations, lock risks, and data loss potential.
Run the migration up and down against a test database to verify correctness and reversibility.
success_criteria: Up migration applies without errors on a clean test database.
Down migration fully reverts the up migration.
Destructive operations (DROP, TRUNCATE) are explicitly flagged in the safety report.
Migration follows project naming and ordering conventions.

items.summary: Reconcile documentation with actual code behavior after a change and remove stale guidance.
triggers: update the docs for this change
steps: Check the current docs against the changed code paths and identify stale statements or missing examples.
Update only the affected documentation sections and preserve existing repo style.
Confirm that commands, APIs, and examples still match current code behavior.
success_criteria: Updated docs describe the current code accurately.
Examples or commands in edited sections remain valid.
Stale guidance introduced by the change is removed.

items.summary: Diagnose a failing build from logs, apply the smallest credible fix, and rerun the build path.
steps: Identify the first actionable error and map it to the owning files or dependency edge.
Change only the code or config necessary to address the confirmed root cause.
Rerun the failing command and then the closest relevant follow-on validation.
success_criteria: The original failing build step passes or a new blocker is identified with evidence.
The fix is scoped to the root cause rather than broad cleanup.
Verification results are recorded.

items.summary: Create a new frontend component with props interface, styling, accessibility attributes, and unit tests following project conventions.
triggers: scaffold a UI component with tests
add a new component to the design system
implement this component from the design
steps: Inspect existing components to identify naming patterns, folder structure, styling approach, and prop conventions.
Define the component props interface, variants, and public API surface based on the spec.
Write the component source with accessibility attributes, keyboard handling, and responsive behavior.
Apply styling using the project convention (CSS modules, Tailwind, styled-components, etc.).
Write unit and rendering tests covering props, variants, accessibility, and interaction handlers.
Run the test suite and lint checks to confirm the component passes all validation.
success_criteria: Component renders correctly with all documented prop variants.
Accessibility attributes (ARIA, keyboard nav) are in place.
Unit tests cover rendering, interaction, and edge cases.
Code follows existing project conventions for structure and naming.

items.summary: Profile a slow code path, identify bottlenecks, apply targeted optimizations, and verify measurable improvement with benchmarks.
steps: Capture baseline metrics (latency, throughput, memory) for the target code path under realistic load.
Run profiling tools to identify hot spots, allocations, and time distribution across the code path.
Analyze profile data to pinpoint the root cause of slowness (N+1 queries, excessive allocations, blocking I/O, etc.).
Implement the smallest targeted fix addressing the confirmed bottleneck (caching, batching, indexing, memoization).
Rerun the same workload and compare metrics against the baseline to quantify improvement.
Run the full test suite to confirm the optimization does not break correctness.
success_criteria: Baseline and post-optimization metrics are recorded and comparable.
The optimization targets the confirmed bottleneck, not speculative changes.
No test regressions are introduced.
Improvement meets or exceeds the stated performance target if one was provided.

items.summary: Plan and execute a multi-file refactoring campaign targeting code smells, architectural debt, or pattern modernization with incremental verification.
triggers: refactor this module to reduce complexity
clean up the code smells in this area
modernize the patterns in this subsystem
extract shared logic into a utility
improve the architecture of this service
run a refactoring campaign on this domain
steps: Analyze the target scope for code smells, duplication, complexity metrics, and architectural issues.
Create a prioritized refactoring plan with small, independently verifiable changes ordered by impact and risk.
Confirm existing tests pass and capture a baseline before any changes are made.
Apply refactoring changes in small increments, running tests after each batch to catch regressions early.
Run a code review pass on the refactored code to verify the improvements and catch any introduced issues.
Run the complete test suite, lint, and type checks on the full refactored codebase.
Document what was refactored, complexity deltas, remaining debt, and any deferred items.
success_criteria: Refactoring produces measurable improvements in complexity, duplication, or coupling.
No behavioral changes are introduced (tests pass unchanged).
Changes are applied incrementally with validation between batches.
Remaining debt and deferred items are documented.

items.summary: Build a practical mental model of an unfamiliar repository before making changes.
triggers: get me oriented in this repo
steps: Read the repo root, build scripts, and main runtime entrypoints to locate the control flow.
Group the main packages or directories by responsibility and note the important interfaces.
Call out unclear ownership, validation gaps, and likely hotspots tied to the user goal.
success_criteria: The overview names the real entrypoints and major modules.
The output helps choose a next change path.
Risk notes are grounded in observed files rather than guesses.

items.summary: Deliver a feature or bug fix using red-green-refactor with tests written before production changes.
steps: Add or update the smallest test that captures the missing behavior and confirm it fails for the expected reason.
Implement only enough production code to satisfy the failing test.
Clean local design debt without changing behavior, then rerun the focused suite.
success_criteria: A failing test exists before the implementation change.
The relevant tests pass after the implementation.
Refactor changes do not expand beyond the feature surface.

items.summary: Identify coverage gaps and add targeted tests to expand the test suite with meaningful assertions and edge case coverage.
triggers: write missing tests for this area
steps: Run or ingest the coverage report and identify untested branches, functions, and edge cases.
Rank gaps by risk and business impact to determine which tests to add first.
Inspect existing tests for patterns, helpers, mocking approach, and assertion style.
Add tests for the prioritized gaps following project conventions and meaningful assertion patterns.
Rerun the test suite and coverage tool to confirm the delta and that all new tests pass.
Summarize coverage improvement, remaining gaps, and any tests that were intentionally deferred.
success_criteria: Coverage increases measurably for the target module.
New tests follow existing project conventions and patterns.
No flaky or always-passing tests are introduced.
Remaining gaps are documented with rationale for deferral.

items.summary: Prepare for a financial audit including document organization, control testing, workpaper preparation, and findings pre-assessment.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve documentation standards and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The preparation covers all audit scope areas with organized documentation.
Key controls are pre-tested with results and gap identification.
The final output explicitly states it is preparation support and requires human audit owner review.

items.summary: Develop an annual or periodic budget with department-level detail, strategic alignment, assumption documentation, and approval workflow.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve assumption documentation and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The budget includes department-level detail with documented assumptions.
Major allocations are mapped to strategic priorities.
The final output explicitly states it is a planning document and requires human budget owner approval.

items.summary: Execute a structured financial analysis process including statement review, ratio analysis, trend identification, and findings documentation.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve source labeling and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The report covers all available financial statements with material findings.
Key ratios are calculated, interpreted, and benchmarked where data is available.
The final output explicitly states it is not financial advice and requires human finance review before operational use.

items.summary: Build a financial model with three-statement integration, assumption documentation, scenario analysis, and sensitivity tables.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve assumption documentation and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The model integrates all three financial statements with proper linking.
All material assumptions are documented with sources and rationale.
The final output explicitly states it is decision support and requires human review before operational use.

items.summary: Evaluate investment opportunities through fundamental analysis, risk assessment, and portfolio fit analysis with decision support output.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve disclaimers and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The evaluation covers fundamental analysis, valuation, and risk assessment.
Scenarios are included with explicit assumptions for each case.
The final output explicitly states it is not investment advice and requires human review before any investment decision.

items.summary: monthly-close-support workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Prepare a monthly forecast package with assumptions, budget variance, and cash posture for finance-owner review.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve source labeling and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The package labels actuals, forecast assumptions, and scenario ranges without ambiguity.
The forecast includes variance commentary and cash implications, not just projected totals.
The final output explicitly states it is not financial advice and requires human finance review before operational use.

items.summary: Conduct a structured financial risk assessment including identification, scoring, mitigation planning, and risk register updates.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve risk categorization and reviewer handoff.

success_criteria: The assessment identifies and categorizes risks across all relevant categories.
Each risk is scored with likelihood and impact, and residual risk is assessed.
The final output explicitly states it is decision support and requires human risk owner review.

items.summary: Analyze clinical datasets for outcomes, utilization, and quality insights with de-identification verification and statistical rigor.
success_criteria: De-identification status verified before any data access.
Statistical methods are appropriate for the study design and data type.
Data quality limitations are explicitly stated.
Findings include confidence intervals and clinical significance.
Recommendations are specific and actionable.

items.summary: Screen a clinical guideline or care pathway for evidence quality, safety constraints, and implementation readiness before clinician review.
success_criteria: Every recommendation in the guideline has an evidence grade.
Safety concerns and contraindications are explicitly flagged.
The output ends with a clinician handoff, not approval language.
No PHI appears in the output.

items.summary: clinical-note-summary workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Conduct a comprehensive HIPAA compliance audit covering Privacy Rule, Security Rule, and Breach Notification requirements.
success_criteria: Every HIPAA requirement is mapped to a control or identified as a gap.
Risk assessment is current and covers all ePHI systems.
Remediation plan is prioritized by risk with timelines and owners.
No PHI appears in any audit output.
All Business Associate Agreements are validated.

items.summary: Design and execute a healthcare quality improvement project using PDSA cycles with baseline metrics and sustainability planning.
success_criteria: Aim statement is specific, measurable, and time-bound.
Baseline metrics are established with appropriate statistical methods.
Interventions are based on evidence or best practices.
PDSA cycles include predictions, data collection plans, and analysis.
Sustainability plan addresses spread to other units or sites.

items.summary: Plan and evaluate a telehealth implementation including platform selection, workflow design, regulatory compliance, and patient access.
success_criteria: HIPAA compliance verified with Business Associate Agreement in place.
Multi-state licensure requirements addressed for all practice states.
Patient access barriers assessed with mitigation strategies.
Clinical workflows documented for each visit type.
Implementation plan includes provider training and go-live support.

items.summary: Design or refresh a compensation analysis with benchmarking, pay equity review, and total rewards recommendations.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve pay equity and human decision ownership.

success_criteria: The analysis can be reviewed without guessing market data sources or methodology.
Pay equity findings are clear enough to inform compensation decisions.
The output clearly states that individual pay decisions remain with human managers.

items.summary: Design or refine a hiring pipeline with role criteria, candidate stages, and human-review checkpoints.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve fairness and human decision ownership.

success_criteria: The pipeline can be executed without guessing stage purpose or ownership.
Evaluation guidance is structured and consistent enough to reduce ad hoc decision making.
The output clearly states that hiring, policy, and offer decisions remain with human managers or HR leads.

items.summary: offer-letter-prep workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Design or refresh an onboarding process with ramp plans, checklists, and manager handoff checkpoints.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve new hire experience and human decision ownership.

success_criteria: The onboarding plan can be executed without guessing ramp goals or step ownership.
Manager handoff is clear enough for consistent new hire experience.
The output clearly states that policy and compliance decisions remain with human managers.

items.summary: Design or refresh a performance review cycle with evaluation frameworks, calibration processes, and growth plan templates.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve fairness, consistency, and human decision ownership.

success_criteria: The review cycle can be executed without guessing evaluation criteria or calibration steps.
Calibration guidance is structured enough to ensure consistency across managers.
The output clearly states that individual performance outcomes remain with human managers.

items.summary: Design or refresh a recruitment process with sourcing strategy, candidate evaluation, and human-review checkpoints.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve fairness and human decision ownership.

success_criteria: The recruitment plan can be executed without guessing role criteria or sourcing strategy.
Evaluation guidance is structured and consistent enough to reduce ad hoc decision making.
The output clearly states that hiring and policy decisions remain with human managers.

items.summary: Design an MCP server or connector shape by grounding requirements in source systems, tool contracts, and host architecture.
triggers: design an MCP server for this API
work out the tool contract for an MCP integration
steps: Inspect official API docs, auth constraints, quotas, and object model for the target system.
Define the minimum useful MCP tools, inputs, outputs, and failure semantics for the user jobs.
Check hosting, caching, pagination, and observability choices against the current control-plane architecture.
success_criteria: The proposed tool surface is small, coherent, and grounded in vendor capabilities.
Authentication, rate limits, and pagination behavior are specified.
Architecture notes fit the existing repo direction instead of assuming a new platform.

items.summary: Conduct a compliance audit from framework mapping through gap identification to remediation planning.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

success_criteria: Framework requirements are fully mapped to controls.
Each gap includes severity, rationale, and suggested remediation.
Output ends with remediation plan for compliance-owner review.

items.summary: Systematic contract review from initial triage through clause analysis to attorney handoff.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

success_criteria: Contract is classified and prioritized correctly.
Each flagged clause includes severity, rationale, and suggested action.
Output ends with attorney handoff, not approval language.

items.summary: Screen a contract for clause risks, privacy concerns, and compliance gaps before attorney or policy-owner review.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

success_criteria: The deliverable lists observed risks by clause area without presenting final legal conclusions.
Privacy and compliance findings are explicit enough for a specialist reviewer to follow up.
The output ends with unresolved questions and a human-review handoff rather than approval language.

items.summary: Conduct legal due diligence from document collection through risk flagging to attorney review.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

success_criteria: Diligence scope covers all required categories.
Each risk flag includes severity, rationale, and next steps.
Output ends with attorney review packet, not deal recommendations.

items.summary: Structured legal research from question formulation through precedent analysis to attorney review packet.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

success_criteria: Research question is clearly defined and scoped.
All findings include proper citations with jurisdiction notes.
Output ends with attorney review packet, not legal conclusions.

items.summary: Develop internal policies from requirements gathering through drafting to stakeholder review.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

success_criteria: Policy covers all required regulatory and stakeholder requirements.
Policy is written in plain language with proper structure.
Output ends with review checklist for stakeholder approval.

items.summary: vendor-contract-triage workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Reconcile demand forecasts with inventory posture to identify supply-demand gaps and recommend balancing actions.
success_criteria: The gap analysis shows supply-demand mismatches by period.
Recommended actions are specific to product family and timing.
Key risks and assumptions are explicitly stated.

items.summary: Analyze production performance, identify bottlenecks, and recommend optimization actions to improve throughput, quality, and cost efficiency.
success_criteria: Bottlenecks are identified and ranked by throughput impact.
Optimization actions are specific with expected impact and timeline.
Current state performance baseline is documented.

items.summary: Investigate quality issues, perform root cause analysis, and develop corrective and preventive action plans to reduce defects.
success_criteria: Root causes are validated with evidence, not assumptions.
Corrective actions are specific with owners and timelines.
Current quality baseline is documented with metrics.

items.summary: Analyze supply chain performance, identify risks, and recommend improvements to sourcing, logistics, and supplier management.
success_criteria: Supply chain assessment covers sourcing, logistics, and suppliers.
Risks are ranked with mitigation strategies.
Improvement roadmap is prioritized with ROI estimates.

items.summary: Develop a content marketing strategy with topic clusters, editorial calendar, and distribution plan aligned to business goals.

budget.notes: Focus on strategy alignment and actionable calendar.

success_criteria: Strategy document connects content to business outcomes.
Editorial calendar is realistic and sustainable.
Distribution plan ensures content reaches target audience.

items.summary: content-pipeline workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Systematically improve conversion rates through heuristic analysis, A/B test design, and data-driven optimization.

budget.notes: Focus on data-driven hypotheses and measurable outcomes.

success_criteria: CRO audit identifies key conversion barriers.
Hypotheses are data-backed and prioritized.
Testing roadmap provides clear next steps with expected impact.

items.summary: Design and plan email marketing campaigns with segmentation, personalization, and compliance checks.

budget.notes: Focus on compliant, segmented campaign design.

success_criteria: Campaign brief covers goal, segments, and send plan.
Email copy is personalized and includes clear CTAs.
Compliance checklist confirms legal and deliverability requirements.

items.summary: Plan a launch campaign with channel messaging, SEO support, and execution checkpoints before handoff.

budget.notes: Keep source intake narrow and focus on launch-ready outputs.

success_criteria: A single launch brief covers audience, narrative, channels, and measurement.
Content and SEO recommendations align with the same offer and timeline.
No unsupported claims or missing CTA paths remain in the final handoff.

items.summary: Design and structure paid advertising campaigns across search and social platforms with budget allocation and optimization plan.

budget.notes: Focus on structured campaign design with clear budget logic.

success_criteria: Campaign structure is logical and platform-appropriate.
Budget allocation is justified by expected performance.
Optimization plan includes testing and iteration schedule.

items.summary: Conduct a comprehensive SEO audit covering technical health, content gaps, and competitive positioning with prioritized remediation.

budget.notes: Focus on actionable findings with clear prioritization.

success_criteria: Audit covers technical, on-page, and off-page SEO dimensions.
Issues are prioritized with clear impact and effort estimates.
Remediation roadmap provides actionable next steps.

items.summary: seo-improvement workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Plan and execute social media campaigns with platform-specific content, engagement tactics, and performance tracking.

budget.notes: Focus on platform-specific activation and engagement.

success_criteria: Strategy covers each platform with specific content approach.
Content calendar is actionable with formats and timing.
Measurement plan connects to business outcomes.

items.summary: End-to-end workflow for creating new agents with proper design, validation, dossier, and fixture generation.

budget.notes: Stay focused on agent creation lifecycle only.

success_criteria: Agent specification passes format and content validation.
Dossier score is >= 80 with complete provenance.
Routing fixtures cover positive, edge, and negative cases.

items.summary: End-to-end workflow for generating comprehensive test fixtures for routing, skill, and workflow validation.

budget.notes: Stay focused on fixture generation only.

success_criteria: All fixtures pass format validation.
Coverage requirements met for routing, skill, and workflow fixtures.
Edge cases and negative scenarios included.

items.summary: End-to-end workflow for running quality assurance checks across agents, skills, and workflows.

budget.notes: Stay focused on quality assurance checks only.

success_criteria: All items in audit scope evaluated.
Compliance matrix complete with pass/fail per artifact.
Remediation list prioritized by impact.

items.summary: End-to-end workflow for developing new skills with proper authoring, validation, dossier creation, and fixture generation.

budget.notes: Stay focused on skill development lifecycle only.

success_criteria: Skill specification passes format and content validation.
Dossier score is >= 80 with complete provenance.
Test fixtures cover trigger scenarios and edge cases.

items.summary: Mine candidate sources, evaluate quality and license fit, and assemble a dossier for workflow or agent promotion.

budget.notes: Stay narrow: source quality, provenance, and promotion readiness only.

success_criteria: The source pack prioritizes primary and maintainable references over generic discovery links.
The evaluation summary makes license and duplication risk obvious.
The resulting dossier is promotion-ready for staging without bypassing provenance checks.

items.summary: End-to-end workflow for optimizing system performance through boot token reduction, route table compression, and registry pruning.

budget.notes: Stay focused on system optimization only.

success_criteria: Baseline measured before any changes.
Optimizations applied with measurable improvement.
No functionality regression introduced.

items.summary: End-to-end workflow for designing new workflows with proper step sequencing, agent routing, gates, and validation.

budget.notes: Stay focused on workflow design lifecycle only.

success_criteria: Workflow specification passes format and content validation.
Dossier score is >= 80 with complete provenance.
Test fixtures cover primary and edge case scenarios.

items.summary: Triage a CI failure, isolate the likely cause, and recommend the smallest corrective action with rerun guidance.

budget.notes: Favor evidence-backed root cause isolation over broad rebuilds.

success_criteria: The triage identifies a plausible root cause with evidence from the failing run.
The fix plan is scoped to the failure and includes a safe rerun path.
Secret handling and action-version hygiene are preserved in any recommended change.

items.summary: Plan and execute workload migration between cloud providers or from on-premises to cloud with minimal downtime.
success_criteria: All workloads are migrated with data integrity verified.
Target environment passes functional and performance tests.
Cutover completes within the defined downtime tolerance.
Source environment is safely decommissioned after validation.

items.summary: Design and implement disaster recovery capabilities including backup strategies, failover mechanisms, and recovery testing.
success_criteria: DR architecture covers all critical services with defined RTO/RPO.
Backups are automated, encrypted, and restoration-tested.
Failover mechanism is automated and tested end-to-end.
DR test report confirms RTO/RPO targets are met.

items.summary: Execute a structured incident response workflow from detection through triage, mitigation, communication, and post-mortem.
success_criteria: Incident is classified and response team assembled within SLA.
Mitigation actions reduce measurable impact.
Stakeholders receive timely updates throughout the incident.
Post-mortem is drafted within 24 hours with actionable items.

items.summary: Provision and configure a production-ready Kubernetes cluster with networking, storage, security, and observability foundations.
triggers: setup k8s with networking and storage
success_criteria: Cluster is provisioned with all nodes in Ready state.
Networking, storage, and security are configured per specification.
Conformance tests pass and validation report is generated.
Rollback procedure is documented and tested.

items.summary: Deploy and configure a full observability stack with metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and alerting rules.
success_criteria: Metrics, logs, and traces pipelines are operational end-to-end.
Dashboards render live data for all defined KPIs.
Alert rules are active and route to configured channels.
Validation report confirms all components pass health checks.

items.summary: Plan, validate, and apply Terraform configurations with state management, drift detection, and approval gates.
success_criteria: Terraform configuration validates and plans successfully.
Plan is reviewed and approved per policy before apply.
All resources are deployed and healthy with clean state.
State backup exists and rollback procedure is documented.

items.summary: Run a competitive analysis process to produce actionable competitive intelligence for product decisions.
steps: Map direct, indirect, and emerging competitors in the market.
Collect data on features, pricing, positioning, and market share.
Build comparison matrix and conduct SWOT analysis.
Produce strategic recommendations based on competitive position.
success_criteria: Competitive landscape is comprehensively mapped.
Data sources are cited with recency indicators.
Strategic recommendations are specific and actionable.

items.summary: Run a structured feature prioritization process using frameworks and data to rank the backlog.
steps: Collect feature list, strategic goals, and available data.
Score features using RICE, ICE, or MoSCoW framework.
Validate scores with data, rank features, and document tradeoffs.
success_criteria: Prioritization framework is applied consistently across all features.
Scores are backed by available data and user research.
Tradeoffs are explicitly documented for stakeholder review.

items.summary: prd-review workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Convert a PRD into an execution plan with scope, sequencing, dependencies, and operating constraints.
triggers: turn this PRD into an execution plan
break this product doc into work
convert requirements into an operating plan
steps: Pull the goals, users, requirements, constraints, and non-goals out of the PRD.
Break the work into phases with dependencies, milestones, and decision points.
Check for resourcing, operational, launch, and feedback-loop gaps before finalizing the plan.
success_criteria: The plan is sequenced rather than a flat task list.
Dependencies, risks, and unresolved assumptions are visible.
The output is usable by product and operations without restating the PRD.

items.summary: Run a product discovery cycle from problem identification through validated solution concepts.
steps: Frame the problem space with user evidence and business context.
Conduct user research to validate problem and uncover needs.
Generate and evaluate solution concepts against user needs and feasibility.
Validate top concepts and produce discovery report with recommendations.
success_criteria: Problem is validated with real user data, not assumptions.
Solution concepts are evaluated against user needs and feasibility.
Discovery report provides clear next steps for product decisions.

items.summary: Coordinate a product launch across all functional areas with readiness checks and post-launch monitoring.
steps: Define launch scope, audience, timeline, and cross-functional responsibilities.
Coordinate marketing, docs, support, and engineering readiness.
Conduct go/no-go readiness review with all functional areas.
Execute launch and monitor success metrics with rollback plan ready.
success_criteria: All functional areas have completed readiness checklists.
Go/no-go criteria are met before launch.
Post-launch monitoring is in place with defined metrics.

items.summary: Build a product roadmap aligned to strategy with themes, initiatives, dependencies, and success metrics.
steps: Define strategic themes aligned to business goals.
Map initiatives to themes, estimate effort, and identify dependencies.
Sequence work into time horizons and define success metrics per theme.
Create stakeholder-specific views and communication plan.
success_criteria: Roadmap themes are aligned to strategic goals.
Dependencies are mapped and visible.
Success metrics are defined for each theme.

items.summary: roadmap-refresh workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Plan and execute a user research process from question definition through actionable insights.
steps: Define research questions and select appropriate methodology.
Plan participant recruitment and create research instruments.
Execute research sessions with target participants.
Analyze data, identify themes, and produce actionable insights.
success_criteria: Research questions are clearly defined and answered.
Methodology is appropriate for the questions asked.
Insights are actionable and linked to product decisions.

items.summary: Execute a research data collection process with instrument deployment, participant recruitment, data quality monitoring, and dataset compilation.
success_criteria: Recruitment meets the planned sample size within acceptable margins.
Data quality metrics exceed pre-defined thresholds.
Compiled dataset is documented and ready for analysis.

items.summary: Conduct multi-source research on a topic and produce a cited synthesis report with evidence quality grading.
success_criteria: The report answers the research question with cited evidence.
Source quality is graded so the reader can judge reliability.
Open questions and evidence gaps are explicitly listed.

items.summary: Execute a structured literature review with systematic search, screening, data extraction, and narrative synthesis following PRISMA-aligned methodology.
success_criteria: The review follows a documented and reproducible search strategy.
All included studies are described in a structured evidence table.
The synthesis distinguishes strong from weak evidence.

items.summary: Set up a new research project with protocol development, ethics planning, data management plan, team coordination, and milestone scheduling.
success_criteria: A complete research protocol document is produced.
Ethics and data management plans meet institutional requirements.
Project timeline has clear milestones with dependencies mapped.

items.summary: Manage the research publication process from manuscript drafting through journal selection, submission preparation, peer review response, and dissemination.
success_criteria: Manuscript is formatted for the target journal and passes initial editorial screening.
All citations are verified and correctly formatted.
Submission package is complete with all required documents.

items.summary: Conduct a full PRISMA-compliant systematic review with protocol registration, dual screening, data extraction, risk-of-bias assessment, and synthesis.
success_criteria: PRISMA flow diagram accounts for every record from search to inclusion.
Data extraction covers all pre-specified fields.
Synthesis includes risk of bias and evidence quality assessment.

items.summary: Build a strategic account plan with stakeholder mapping, expansion opportunities, and risk assessment for key accounts.

budget.notes: Focus on evidence-based expansion and risk mitigation.

success_criteria: The account plan has specific quarterly objectives and actions.
Stakeholder map includes relationship status and influence level.
Expansion opportunities are evidence-based with timeline.

items.summary: Manage commercial contract negotiation with term analysis, concession planning, and legal routing for non-standard terms.

budget.notes: Focus on protecting value while enabling deal progress.

success_criteria: The negotiation strategy protects value while enabling deal progress.
Concession limits are defined with approval requirements.
Non-standard terms are routed to legal for review.

items.summary: crm-hygiene workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Review an active deal for stage health, win blockers, and proposal readiness before next action.

budget.notes: Bias toward concrete blockers and near-term next steps.

success_criteria: The review distinguishes deal evidence from assumptions.
The next-step plan is specific enough for immediate seller follow-up.
Proposal changes clearly reinforce the active deal strategy.

items.summary: Develop a persuasive proposal aligned to buyer requirements with clear value proposition, scope, and commercial terms.

budget.notes: Bias toward buyer-aligned narrative and clear commercial terms.

success_criteria: The proposal addresses all buyer requirements with specific solutions.
Pricing is clear, transparent, and policy-compliant.
Open issues are documented for buyer discussion.

items.summary: proposal-review workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Build a sales forecast using pipeline data, historical close rates, and deal-level analysis for revenue planning.

budget.notes: Focus on data-driven forecast with clear assumptions.

success_criteria: The forecast uses stage-weighted pipeline with historical close rates.
Top opportunities have deal-level confidence assessments.
Forecast risks and upside opportunities are documented.

items.summary: Qualify a lead or opportunity using structured frameworks to produce a scored recommendation and next-step plan.

budget.notes: Focus on evidence-based scoring and clear next steps.

success_criteria: The qualification score distinguishes evidence from assumptions.
Next steps are specific enough for immediate seller follow-up.
Disqualification criteria are explicitly checked.

items.summary: Execute a structured incident response playbook from detection through containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review.
steps: Validate the incident, classify severity, and assemble the response team with clear roles.
Apply short-term containment to limit blast radius while preserving forensic evidence.
Analyze logs, artifacts, and attack vectors to determine root cause and scope of compromise.
Remove attacker access, patch exploited vulnerabilities, and clean compromised systems.
Restore systems from known-good state, validate integrity, and monitor for re-compromise.
Document lessons learned, update detection rules, and produce a final incident report.
Update response playbooks and runbooks based on gaps identified during the incident.
success_criteria: Incident is classified and triaged within defined SLA.
Containment actions are applied and documented before eradication begins.
Root cause analysis is supported by evidence, not speculation.
Recovery includes validation that systems are clean and functional.
Post-incident report includes actionable improvements.

items.summary: Execute a structured penetration test following reconnaissance, exploitation, post-exploitation, and reporting phases.
triggers: test our defenses with a pentest
steps: Validate scope, rules of engagement, and authorization before any testing begins.
Gather information about targets through passive and active reconnaissance techniques.
Identify potential vulnerabilities through scanning, fingerprinting, and manual testing.
Attempt to exploit identified vulnerabilities to demonstrate real-world impact.
Assess lateral movement, privilege escalation, and data access capabilities.
Document findings with evidence, risk ratings, and actionable remediation guidance.
Present findings to stakeholders and answer technical questions about the assessment.
success_criteria: All testing stays within authorized scope and rules of engagement.
Vulnerabilities are demonstrated with proof-of-concept evidence.
Risk ratings reflect real-world exploitability and business impact.
Remediation recommendations are specific and actionable.
Final report is suitable for both technical and executive audiences.

items.summary: Review an LLM feature for prompt injection exposure across trust boundaries, retrieved content, and tool use.
triggers: review this agent for prompt injection
audit the LLM flow for prompt attacks
check this RAG system for prompt injection risk
steps: Identify where user input, retrieved content, external tools, and system prompts cross trust boundaries.
Model direct and indirect prompt injection paths including retrieval, tool output, and memory contamination.
Check whether the implementation actually enforces the proposed mitigations.
success_criteria: The review names concrete prompt injection paths rather than generic LLM risk language.
Mitigations are tied to specific trust boundaries or code controls.
Any missing isolation or sanitization controls are clearly prioritized.

items.summary: Assess a repository for security risk by combining baseline code review with dedicated secret scanning.
triggers: scan this repo for security risks
steps: Run deterministic security scanners first and capture their raw outputs.
Sweep the same scope for committed secrets, tokens, keys, and credential patterns.
Read the flagged code paths, drop false positives, and rank confirmed issues by severity.
success_criteria: Confirmed findings include severity, evidence, and remediation direction.
Secret exposure results are reported separately from general code risks.
False positives are explicitly filtered or explained.

items.summary: Conduct a comprehensive security audit covering code, dependencies, infrastructure config, and access controls.
triggers: audit the entire codebase for security
conduct a security review of the platform
steps: Define audit boundaries, identify critical assets, and establish risk tolerance thresholds.
Run SAST tools across the codebase to identify code-level vulnerabilities and insecure patterns.
Scan all dependencies for known CVEs, license risks, and outdated packages.
Assess infrastructure-as-code configs, secrets management, network policies, and access controls.
Sweep repositories and configs for leaked credentials, tokens, keys, and hardcoded secrets.
Aggregate findings, assign CVSS-aligned risk scores, and produce a prioritized remediation roadmap.
success_criteria: Every finding includes severity, evidence path, and remediation guidance.
Risk matrix covers all scanned areas with no unexplained gaps.
Remediation roadmap is ordered by risk and includes effort estimates.
Compliance gaps are mapped to specific framework controls.

items.summary: Verify security controls against compliance frameworks and identify gaps requiring remediation.
steps: Map compliance framework requirements to existing security controls and policies.
Evaluate each control for design effectiveness and operational implementation.
Gather and validate evidence demonstrating control effectiveness and compliance.
Identify missing controls, weak implementations, and insufficient evidence.
Prioritize gaps by compliance risk, business impact, and remediation effort.
Create a phased plan to address gaps and achieve compliance certification readiness.
success_criteria: Every framework requirement is mapped to a control or identified as a gap.
Control assessments distinguish between design and operational effectiveness.
Evidence gaps are explicitly called out with collection guidance.
Remediation plan is prioritized and includes effort estimates.
Certification readiness score reflects current posture accurately.

items.summary: Execute a hypothesis-driven threat hunting campaign to detect advanced threats that evade automated controls.
steps: Develop a testable hypothesis based on threat intelligence, attack patterns, or environmental gaps.
Gather relevant logs, network flows, endpoint data, and other telemetry for analysis.
Apply analytical techniques to search for indicators of the hypothesized threat.
Validate suspicious findings to distinguish true positives from benign anomalies.
Extract indicators of compromise and develop detection rules for ongoing monitoring.
Document findings, create detection rules, and hand off confirmed threats to incident response.
success_criteria: Hunting hypothesis is specific, testable, and tied to a threat model.
Data collection covers relevant sources without excessive noise.
Findings are validated with evidence before escalation.
IOCs are actionable and suitable for detection rule creation.
Hunting report includes methodology, findings, and recommendations.

items.summary: Remediate identified vulnerabilities through patching, configuration changes, or compensating controls with verification.
steps: Confirm vulnerability exists, is exploitable, and assess current exposure in the environment.
Evaluate business impact, affected systems, and potential blast radius of exploitation.
Select remediation approach: patch, configuration change, workaround, or compensating control.
Apply patches or configuration changes in a controlled manner with rollback capability.
Verify the fix resolves the vulnerability and does not introduce regressions or new issues.
Document any remaining risk, accepted exceptions, and monitoring enhancements.
success_criteria: Vulnerability is confirmed and prioritized before remediation begins.
Patch or fix is applied without breaking existing functionality.
Verification confirms the vulnerability is resolved.
Residual risk and exceptions are explicitly documented.

items.summary: Validate a startup business model through Lean Canvas creation, customer discovery, market sizing, and financial viability assessment.
success_criteria: Lean Canvas is complete with evidence-backed entries.
Customer discovery findings support or refute key hypotheses.
Market sizing provides realistic opportunity assessment.

items.summary: Assemble a structured CEO review or office-hours decision pack with priorities, blockers, and recommended decisions.
success_criteria: The pack separates decisions-needed from status updates.
Every blocker has an owner and a proposed unblocking step.
The output is concise enough for executive consumption without further editing.

items.summary: Conduct structured customer discovery to validate problem-solution fit through interviews, synthesis, and hypothesis testing.
success_criteria: Interview guide follows Mom Test methodology.
Findings are synthesized across interviews with clear patterns.
Hypothesis validation verdict is evidence-based and actionable.

items.summary: Prepare for a fundraising round with strategy, materials, investor targeting, and outreach planning.
success_criteria: Fundraising readiness is assessed with clear gaps identified.
Investor targets are matched to thesis, stage, and check size.
All required materials are listed with ownership and status.

items.summary: fundraising-readiness workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met

items.summary: Create an investor-ready pitch deck with compelling narrative, data visualization, and structured storytelling.
success_criteria: Deck follows proven pitch narrative structure.
All key claims are backed by data or evidence.
The ask is clear with specific amount and use of funds.

items.summary: weekly-operating-review workflow
success_criteria: workflow_objectives_met
